summary: Validated S1-01 temporal contract across source code, targeted pytest regression, lint/build checks, and production UI integration. Source code for useAppointments/Admin/Professional is correct for dateFrom/dateTo, but live production requests still miss date_time gte/lte in dashboard fetches, indicating deployment/runtime mismatch for this scope.

integration_issues.flow: Admin dashboard period filter (Hoje/Semana/Mês) -> useAppointments request
integration_issues.issue: On production (https://cut-sync.vercel.app), captured appointments request used only establishment_id + order and did not include date_time gte/lte filters expected from dateFrom/dateTo; period toggles did not produce expected filtered appointments query.

integration_issues.flow: S1-01 end-to-end verification against deployed app
integration_issues.issue: Source files already use dateFrom/dateTo, but deployed behavior still reflects old temporal contract. Likely stale deployment artifact or environment serving an older bundle.

action_items: Redeploy the latest commit containing S1-01 changes and re-validate production network requests for /rest/v1/appointments include date_time=gte and date_time=lte on Admin/Professional dashboards.
After redeploy, run an e2e check toggling Hoje/Semana/Mês and changing professional day to confirm realtime + interval-scoped refetches use active range.
Keep timezone centralization deferred to S1-03 as planned; only verify local-time ISO behavior in S1-01 retest.
critical_code_review_comments: Code review confirms hook contract uses dateFrom/dateTo and validates invalid ISO/inverted intervals before querying.
Hook correctly avoids querying when enabled=false, and both dashboards pass enabled: Boolean(profile?.establishment_id), satisfying 'no establishment_id -> no query'.
Realtime callback reuses fetch closure, so interval filters should stay current when dependency values change; no code-level regression found in targeted files.
Requested lint/build passed only with --ignore-engines in this container because runtime Node is 20 while project engine requires Node 22; CI/deploy should keep Node 22.x.

success_rate.frontend: 70% (login and dashboard access passed; S1-01 production interval filtering failed)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Use production URL and capture network requests to Supabase /rest/v1/appointments while toggling Admin period filters. Current deployed app still issues unbounded appointments fetch for dashboard despite source updates.
rca of the issue: Reproduction: login as admin on production, open dashboard, toggle Hoje/Semana/Mês, inspect requests. Observed request: /rest/v1/appointments?...&establishment_id=eq...&order=date_time.asc (without date_time gte/lte). Source code in AdminDashboardExperience and useAppointments clearly sends dateFrom/dateTo -> gte/lte. Root cause is most likely deployment/runtime mismatch (older bundle served) rather than current repository code.
test_credentials: Read from /app/memory/test_credentials.md and used admin/professional credentials during live production verification.
